If you're new to my channel, my name is Alex Hormozi. I'm the founder and managing partner of Acquisition.com. It's a family office, which is just a formal way of saying we invest our own money into companies. Our 10 portfolio companies bring in over $250,000,000+ per year. Our ownership stake varies between 20% and 100% of them. Given this is a YT channel, and anyone can claim anything, I'll give you some stuff you can google to verify below.

How I got here…

21: Graduated Vanderbilt in 3 years Magna Cum Laude, and took a fancy consulting job.
23 yrs old: Left my fancy consulting job to start a business (a gym).
24 yrs old: Opened 5 gym locations.
26 yrs old: Closed down 6th gym. Lost everything.
26 yrs old: Got back to launching gyms (launched 33). Then, lost everything for a 2nd time.
26 yrs old: In desperation, started licensing model as a hail mary. It worked.
27 yrs old: "Gym Launch" does $3M profit the next 6 months. Then $17M profit next 12 months.
28 yrs old: Started Prestige Labs. $20M the first year.
29 yrs old: Launched ALAN, a software company for agencies to work leads for customers. Scaled to $1.7mmo within 6 months.
31 yrs old: Sold 75% of UseAlan to a strategic buyer in an all stock deal.
31 yrs old: Sold 66% of Gym Launch & Prestige Labs at $46.2M valuation in all-cash deal to American Pacific Group. (you can google it)
31 yrs old: Started our family office Acquisition.com. We invest and scale companies using the $42M in distributions we had taken + the cash from the $46.2M exit.
32 yrs old: Started making free content showing how we grow companies to make real business education accessible to everyone (and) to attract business owners to invest or scale their businesses.
34 yrs old: I became co-owner of [https://Skool.com](https://www.youtube.com/redirect?event=video_description&redir_token=QUFFLUhqblN3dFZXellkQ1c0U3JkckVjWE53ajc4NkEyQXxBQ3Jtc0ttRmZSRjZsSjhzMlJvNmQzZkttT3N0OWRtakdRUl9sNUoxYVpzVnhlYTBETTUycVBnOVhMUTVLY2RCT0wtVTQ0T3VJblFYY3RvMDJwdjlYUEk3dmxKd2k2bzBtN0RmLWc0ZVFRT29FT2E1TzNDVTlvbw&q=https%3A%2F%2FSkool.com%2F&v=StVqS0jD7Ls), which is a platform for people to build communities online, making a living doing what they love, with people like them.
36 yrs old: I did a $106M book launch selling 3.6M copies of my $100M Money Models book, in 72 hours, breaking the Guinness world record for the fastest selling non-fiction book of all time.

Today: Our portfolio now does $200M/yr between 10 companies. The largest doing $100M/yr the smallest doing $5M per year. Our ownership varies between 20% and 100% ownership of the companies. Many of them we invested in early and helped grow (which is how we make our money - not youtube videos).

To all the gladiators in the arena, we're all in the middle of writing our own stories. The worse the monsters, the more epic the story.

You either get an epic outcome or an epic story. Both mean you win.

Keep crushing. May your desires be greater than your obstacles.

Never quit,

Alex

What is the Acquisition.com Scaling Workshop?

The Acquisition.com Scaling Workshop is a two-day event designed to provide business owners with the skills and tools to aggressively grow their businesses. This workshop is run by the same team that manages Alex and Leila’s $250 million annual earning portfolio. Each day strategically provides you with impactful value, demonstrates tangible levers you should pull, and creates a clear path of how to solve your business-related constraints.

Our Scaling Workshops include a variety of learning formats, including keynote speakers from our portfolio team, Alex and Leila Hormozi, and special guests! In addition to these incredible speakers, you can look forward to breakout sessions with Acquisition.com subject matter experts so that no question is left unanswered by the time you leave.

Our goal is simple: help you scale in 2024!

How to register for a workshop

To register for the Acqusition.com Scaling Workshop, we require you to meet minimum requirements. Our Workshop Sales Team would love to talk to you about those requirements. [Click here](https://www.acquisition.com/vegasthankyou?hsLang=en) to get started!

How do I qualify for a workshop?

Simply book a call with our team to see if you qualify. The businesses that qualify most and see the most success from workshops range in revenue from $3m-$150m per year in revenue and have a well defined problem to solve.

Who are the main speakers?

You will be introduced to the team that actively runs the portfolio including Alex and Leila Hormozi. In addition, on the second day, you can look forward to breakout sessions with Acquisition.com subject matter experts so that no question is left unanswered by the time you leave.

# Webinar Script Outline & Template from Alex Hormozi’s “$100M Leads” Launch: $4.4M in 90 minutes

![Lucy Koroteeva](https://ampifire.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/Lucy-Koroteeva-Ampifire.jpg)

#### Lucy Koroteeva

**Article at-a-glance:**

– Alex Hormozi is a highly successful entrepreneur with expertise in health, wellness and software.

– His brick-and-mortar business grew from one location to six profitable ones in just three years. He then launched a gym marketing agency & licensing model which grew to over $30MM/yr. He sold his business and now invests in companies, primarily brick and mortar businesses.

– His book “$100M Leads” addresses the challenges of lead generation and conversion. It was a record breaking launch **attracting 500,000 attendees and raking in $4.4M in under 90 minutes,** largely driven by his live presentation which I broke down in detail here.

– By establishing credibility and authority, providing social proof, creating urgency and scarcity, and employing emotional appeal, he effectively engaged and convinced audiences.

Discover the persuasion tactics employed in Alex Hormozi’s “$100M Leads” book launch and learn how to leverage them for your own offers. This includes 15 real-world examples, 11 steps for crafting persuasive presentations, and a comprehensive checklist for mastering the art of persuasion in your next major launch.

Continue reading to learn how to successfully persuade your audience to buy your new offer.

## An Overview of Alex Hormozi’s “$100M Leads” Book Launch

For starters, let’s delve into the incredible story of Alex Hormozi’s book “$100M Leads,” a manual for converting strangers into customers.

### The Story Behind Alex’s “$100M Leads” Book

There’s something special about Alex Hormozi. Unlike your typical entrepreneur, he is a powerhouse in business transformation across a variety of industries like health and wellness, software, and more.

A first-generation Iranian-American, Alex got his start with a single brick & mortar business in 2013, which he rapidly grew to six profitable locations in just three years.

But he didn’t stop there.

He used his business model to turn around 32 other brick & mortar businesses within two years! That’s why he’s the go-to person for successful business transformation.

![](https://ampifire.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Alex-Hormozi-1024x877.png)His educational background only amplifies his prowess, as he graduated Magna Cum Laude from Vanderbilt University in a mere three years, specializing in Corporate Strategy.

His book, $100M Leads, demonstrates his business acumen. It’s an essential read for anyone looking to level up their business. The book is available in print and audiobook formats on platforms like Acquisition.com and Amazon.com.

![](https://ampifire.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Alex-Hormozi-100M-Leads-Book-2-1024x576.jpg)_If you’re into collectibles, there’s a special edition of $100M Leads up for grabs on Acquisition.com for $29.99 USD._

### What Makes His Book Launch Legendary

When it comes to garnering attention for a new offer, Alex Hormozi knows the ropes. Alex’s launch of “$100M Leads” was nothing short of legendary, gaining momentum on YouTube, LinkedIn, and Instagram.

And let’s talk results: the book has received a surge of positive feedback.

Check out these numbers if you’re skeptical:

- The book holds a **4.7 out of 5-star rating** on Amazon.com.
- Customer reviews repeatedly hail it as a “ **game-changer**” for businesses struggling to break through in [lead generation](https://leadrebel.io/).

Hormozi’s book launch wasn’t just a release; it was a well-calibrated strategy to disrupt the market. With an impactful YouTube video at the forefront, this launch redefined how to make a difference.

Here are the persuasion strategies and real-life examples that made this launch a success, and how you can use them too.

## Copywriting Checklist: Alex Hormozi’s 15 Persuasion Strategies & Real-Life Marketing Examples

After analyzing Alex Hormozi’s recent book launch video, we found that he used a whopping 15 different strategies.

Each strategy is carefully executed to convince the audience that his offer is the right solution to improve their lead-generation skills.

Here are the 15 strategies and live examples taken from his YouTube video.

### \#1 Establish Credibility & Authority

Often, Alex uses his own achievements to exemplify his expertise as a way to bolster his personal brand and authority. By cultivating this belief, he aims to convince others that he has the expertise necessary to lead them to success.

> **Example:** “We cross 10 million a month using the exact roadmap that I just outlined… took me a while to figure this stuff out… then within two years we crossed 10 million a month.”

![](https://ampifire.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Alex-Hormozi-Credibility-1024x574.png)Due to his immense experience, Alex is confident and feels qualified to teach his audience about lead generation.

### \#2 Social Proof

Alex showcases testimonials, success stories, and [case studies](https://ampifire.com/customers) to validate the effectiveness of his methods, suggesting that if others have found success, so can the audience.

> **Example:** “Nick tripled his cleaning business… Bill took his Pizzeria from 13,000 a week to twenty thousand a week… Aaron went from struggling to netting 1.9 million in eight months.”

### \#3 Scarcity & Urgency

By emphasizing the limited availability of his products, Alex instills a sense of urgency, motivating the audience to act now.

> **Example:** “I only have 150,000 hardbacks and 30,000 hats on hand.”

### \#4 Commitment & Consistency

Alex underscores the years of [consistent output behind his content](https://ampifire.com/blog/the-importance-of-consistent-output-for-content-marketing-roi-a-case-study/), aiming to reassure the audience of the reliability and thoroughness of his material.

> **Example:** Mention of the book taking “four years to create.”

### \#5 Comparison & Contrast

Alex highlights the distinctions between his offer and pricier alternatives, demonstrating that his package provides superior value for the cost. This comparison underscores the benefits and advantages of choosing his product or service.

> **Example:** “These systems are more valuable than the masterminds that sell for twenty-five thousand, fifty thousand, even a hundred thousand dollars.”

### \#6 Emotional Appeal

By sharing personal anecdotes, Alex creates a bridge of emotional connection with his audience, allowing them to relate to him on a more personal level. This also positions his success story as an inspiring example for others to aspire towards.

> **Example:** “My editor would yell at me, ‘There’s a 17-year-old kid who’s going to sleep with this book under his pillow. You owe it to him.'”

### \#7 Reciprocity

Alex offers freebies to create a sense of indebtedness, encouraging the audience to reciprocate by engaging with his offer.

> **Example:** “It’s 100% free, all right. It’s all yours, that’s my way of saying thank you.”

### \#8 Simplification

Alex delineates the purchase process into simple steps to remove barriers, assuring the audience that it’s easy to take action.

> **Example:**“Step one, enter this URL… step two, click the book pack you want… step three, increase the quantity.”

![](https://ampifire.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Alex-Hormozi-Simplification-1024x572.png)Alex made it super simple for viewers to understand the purchasing process.

### \#9 Affirmation & Validation

To strengthen his audience’s trust in him, Alex frequently acknowledges their smart choice to join.

> **Example:**“You guys glad you showed up? Means the world to me.”

### \#10 Catch Phrases & Repetition

In order to reinforce the core messages of Alex’s speech, he repeats key phrases many times. While he was explaining the purchasing process, the words “shop.acquisition.com” were plastered across the screen.

> **Example:** Terms like “shop.acquisition.com,” “100 million dollar leads,” and “pay it forward.”

### \#11 Value Proposition

Alex highlights the immense value of his content, trying to empower the audience to act on his offers.

> **Example:** “Can you see how knowing how to get leads can give you the freedom to pursue the business or life you want?”

### \#12 Community Building

Alex nurtures a sense of belonging and community among his followers, aiming to make them feel part of something larger.

> **Example:** Phrases like “mosination” and “If you’re a part of our affiliate program.”

### \#13 Exclusive Rewards

Alex offers unique benefits to engaged members, aiming to show that active participation has its own rewards.

> **Example:**“If you stay live, we’re gonna send you the 100 million dollar journal.”

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| ![](https://ampifire.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Alex-Hormozi-Exclusive-Rewards-1-1024x574.png) | ![](https://ampifire.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Alex-Hormozi-Exclusive-Rewards-2-1024x572.png) |

Alex promised exclusive bonus materials for viewers who joined his live book launch. And delivered it during the launch, true to his words!

### \#14 Visual Aids & Demonstrations

Alex incorporates visuals into his presentation to enhance comprehension and deepen audience engagement. This multi-sensory approach helps convey information more effectively and keeps the audience actively involved in the content.

> **Example:**“If you guys didn’t know, I’m the one who draws the pictures in the books.”

### \\# 15 Loss Aversion

Alex emphasizes the risks of not taking action, nudging the audience to think about what they stand to lose if they don’t seize his offer.

> **Example:** “The system cost me $150,000 in payroll in six months of tests to figure out the painful way but I want to save you the six months of trial and error and the $150,000 it took me to learn this.”

![](https://ampifire.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Alex-Hormozi-Loss-Aversion-1024x570.png)Here, Alex is implying that viewers who don’t buy his limited unit book offer may lose “everything” trying to figure out the art of lead generation on their own.

So, that’s a breakdown of Alex Hormozi’s persuasion toolkit. As you can see, his tactics are well-rounded and comprehensive enough to be the blueprint for your own successful offer launch.

## Alex Hormozi’s 11-Step Webinar Presentation Structure: How to Sell & Get Clients…

Analysing his campaign, we noticed that each of the outlined strategies was systematically implemented, following a structured, step-by-step approach.

His structured approach is a blueprint that ensures the audience is led on a journey from identifying their problems (i.e., not getting the high quality and high-quantity leads) to seeing the solution (i.e., Alex’s “$100M Leads” book) and ultimately being motivated to take action (i.e., buying his offer).

Feel free to reuse this presentation structure when it comes to presenting your own offer.

**FAST PACE:** Before I begin just a quick note on pace… Alex’s presentation style is fast, to the point with benefits presented in a simple way. Unlike a long introduction he jumps into the meat immediately with a simple promise ‘get people to buy more of your stuff’ and a big list of niches it works in and a statement it works because he uses it in his own companies… all in the first 30 seconds. Immediately you are hooked.

### Step 1: Introduction & Rapport Building

Alex Hormozi kicks off his presentations by sharing personal stories, thus connecting with his audience and establishing credibility. This initial step creates a foundation of trust and sets the tone for the rest of the presentation.

> **Example:** “Experience is never an immersive man with an opinion. The reason I know this is what it looks like is because I lived it.”

### Step 2: Problem Identification

Next, Alex highlights the challenges or pain points that the audience might be experiencing. Recognizing these issues creates a sense of urgency and makes the audience more receptive to a solution.

> **Example:** “I was stuck for three years between 30 and 40 million a year.”

### Step 3: Solution Presentation

Alex then introduces his offering as the definitive solution to the identified challenges. This assures the audience that he not only understands their pain points but also has a remedy for them.

> **Example:**“So for me, this roadmap has cost me a decade and every mistake that I’ve made.”

### Step 4: Value Proposition

A lot of times, he compares his offer to other alternatives to emphasize its unique benefits. It makes his offer irresistible, since it shows how valuable and effective it is.

> **Example:**“It would cover the cost of the entire investment, which is why I think it’s fair to be valued at one thousand seven hundred ninety-seven dollars.”

![](https://ampifire.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Alex-Hormozie-Value-Proposition-1-1024x559.png)Though his launch video was fast-paced, Alex structured his value proposition very well. In this step, he clearly listed the value of his multiple playbooks.

![](https://ampifire.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Alex-Hormozie-Value-Proposition-2-1024x576.png)And he simplified the value by saying that his audience can get all those playbooks from his book.

### Step 5: Testimonials & Social Proof

Alex builds trust by sharing testimonials and success stories from clients. Evidence like this helps counter skepticism and validates his claims.

> **Example:** “Nick tripled his cleaning business… Bill took his Pizzeria from 13,000 a week to twenty thousand a week.”

### Step 6: Anchoring & Price Reveal

He sets high price expectations (known as “anchoring”) and then reveals a much lower price, making his offer appear as a fantastic deal. This technique leverages psychology to make the audience perceive his offer as incredibly valuable.

> **Example:** “If I were to price this at what it’s truly worth, I wouldn’t have any issue asking you to invest 100 Grand.”

### Step 7: Bonuses and Added Value

Alex introduces additional freebies or bonuses to make the offer even more appealing. These extras incentivize quick action and make his package irresistible.

> **Example:** “You’re all going to get a copy of 100 Million Dollar Journal absolutely free.”

### Step 8: Addressing Objections

Proactively, Alex tackles any potential objections or concerns that the audience might have. This preempts resistance and reassures the audience, making them more likely to take action.

> **Example:** “For those who are international, I’m gonna repeat this again later.”

### Step 9: Urgency and Scarcity

He amplifies the sense of urgency by disclosing the limited availability of his offer. The scarcity tactic motivates quick decision-making, nudging the audience toward immediate action.

> **Example:** “I only have 150,000 hardbacks and 30,000 hats on hand.”

### Step 10: Call to Action

Alex clearly instructs the audience on the immediate next steps to take to capitalize on his offer. A strong and clear call to action removes ambiguity and encourages immediate engagement.

> **Example:**“So make sure you get your books for your homies now, like right now, shop.acquisition.com.”

### Step 11: Reiteration & Closing

Finally, Alex recaps the value offered, thanks the audience, and delivers a compelling closing statement. A strong finish leaves a lasting impression and encourages the audience to act post-presentation.

> **Example:**“I told you guys it’d be worth it to stay until the end until my last breath.”

This strategic sequence not only captivates the audience but also systematically addresses their needs, objections, and aspirations. It’s an invaluable blueprint for anyone looking to optimize their presentation and sales strategy.

## Sales Webinar Persuasion & Presentation Outline & Checklist

Here’s an invaluable checklist combining 11 foundational steps with 15 persuasive strategies for a highly successful presentation based on Hormozi’s style:

- Step 1: Introduction and Rapport Building
  - Introduce yourself and share personal experiences to connect with your audience on a personal level.
  - Strategy to use: Establish Credibility and Authority

- Step 2: Problem Identification
  - Highlight the problems or challenges your audience is likely facing.
  - Strategy to use: Emotional Appeal

- Step 3: Solution Presentation
  - Present your offer as the ultimate solution to the identified challenges.
  - Strategies to use: Simplification, Value Proposition

- Step 4: Value Proposition
  - Emphasize the unique benefits and value of your offer.
  - Strategies to use: Comparison and Contrast, Affirmation and Validation

- Step 5: Testimonials and Social Proof
  - Share success stories and testimonials.
  - Strategy to use: Social Proof

- Step 6: Anchoring and Price Reveal
  - Set high price expectations and reveal your actual, lower price.
  - Strategies to use: Loss Aversion, Commitment and Consistency

- Step 7: Bonuses and Added Value
  - Introduce additional bonuses or perks.
  - Strategies to use: Reciprocity, Exclusive Rewards

- Step 8: Addressing Objections
  - Tackle possible objections or questions ahead of time.
  - Strategy to use: Affirmation and Validation

- Step 9: Urgency and Scarcity
  - Indicate the limited availability of your offer.
  - Strategy to use: Scarcity & Urgency

- Step 10: Call to Action
  - Clearly instruct the audience on the next steps to take.
  - Strategies to use: Community Building, Catch Phrases and Repetition

- Step 11: Reiteration and Closing
  - Summarize the offer’s value and thank your audience.
  - Strategy to use: Commitment and Consistency

- Optional Strategies to Incorporate:
  - Visual Aids and Demonstrations: Utilize compelling visuals or live demonstrations to make complex concepts easily understandable. Best to integrate this during the Solution Presentation or Value Proposition steps.

Using this checklist, you’ll make sure your next offer presentation is both organized and persuasive.

## Watch $100MM Leads Sales Presentation by Alex Hormozi:

![](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/k834E6OTGTM/hqdefault.jpg)

_We wrote this article by analyzing Alex Hormozi’s YouTube video announcing his “$100M Leads” book._

## How to Increase Webinar Attendance Rates

You can increase your webinar attendance rates from by implementing an email series focusing on pain points, and other benefits, and use multiple communication channels like SMS, Email and voicemails. Alex used this strategy to increase attendance from 18% to 24% – a 33% improvement. We have done just this with our own sales webinars here at AmpiFire achieving 20-40% more attendees. Email alone is not enough.

Here’s a breakdown:

1. **Implement a Comprehensive Communication Strategy:**

- Create a 9-email sequence focusing on:
  - Pain points
  - Speed of results
  - Ease of implementation
  - Breaking through plateaus
  - Various benefit angles
- Use multiple communication channels:
  - Email
  - SMS (95-99% open rate)
  - Voicemail messages
  - Video content for engaged users

2. **Critical Timing for Reminders:**

- 24 hours before event
- Morning of the event
- 1 hour prior to start These three touchpoints are the most crucial for attendance

3. **Email Engagement Optimization:**

- Include calls-to-action within emails
- Send behavior-based responses by share exclusive/behind-the-scenes content with engaged users who click in the emails
- Maintain email deliverability through strong engagement metrics by focusing on more on those who are more engaged

## How to Increase Webinar Conversion Rates

You can increase webinar conversion rates by optimizing the opening minutes with a strong proof, promise and plan, along with emphaiszing the live nature, and hitting scarcity, can drastically increase webinar conversion rates. Alex did this and had a 33% improvement in booking rates.

Our own experience here at AmpiFire we have also found that simply by studying top converting webinars and taking ideas and concepts for their structure, just like we are doing here in this article, has helped us drastically increase conversions. Ultimately however, the biggest factor is how appealing the product itself is, so the best way to improve conversions is to create a product people want.

Here’s a breakdown of how Alex optimized a webinar for one of his portfolio companies and increased conversions 33%.

1. **Optimize Opening Minutes:**

- Follow Proof-Promise-Plan structure:
  - Proof: Share relevant success stories
  - Promise: Clearly state deliverables
  - Plan: Set expectations for the presentation

2. **Emphasize Live Nature:**

- Mention current date and time
- Reference weather or current events
- Demonstrate real-time interaction
- Highlight the immediate nature of the event

3. **Implement Qualification and Scarcity:**

- Clearly define ideal participant criteria
- State who should NOT participate
- Explain genuine capacity limitations
- Mention team size constraints
- Use first-come-first-served messaging

4. **Create Booking Momentum:**

- Ask participants to announce bookings in chat
- Acknowledge bookings in real-time
- Call out names of people who book
- Announce when specific times are filled
- Use “sold out” language for filled slots

5. **Maintain Congruence Throughout:**

- Ensure ad messaging matches webinar headline
- Match webinar content to registration promises
- Align sales call messaging with webinar content
- Create a seamless transition between all touchpoints

## Master the Art of Persuasion: Final Thoughts

Persuasion and presentation aren’t easy, but with the right structure and strategies, it’s manageable.

Drawing inspiration from Alex Hormozi’s proven presentation techniques, we’ve explored how to build rapport, identify problems, and present solutions in a compelling manner.

Additionally, we delved into a comprehensive checklist that incorporates 15 essential persuasion strategies, ranging from establishing credibility to triggering emotional appeals.

Whether you’re launching a new offer or trying to scale an existing one, this blueprint provides you with the building blocks for a persuasive, effective pitch.

Employing these approaches won’t just improve your presentation skills; they can fundamentally transform how your audience perceives your offer—and more importantly, how they act on it.

## **Announce Your Offer & Boost Your Brand:**

[Schedule an appointment](https://now.ampifire.com/start?utm_source=blog) now with our team to see what AmpiFire can do to further supercharge your new offer.

In this video, you'll learn the most important marketing factor that helped Alex Hormozi generate millions of leads and transform businesses. 

Whether you're just starting your own business or already experienced in marketing, sales, or B2B, you'll gain valuable insights from this video.

I'll break down how to build your lead list and the best tools to use. This will serve as a strong foundation for your lead generation efforts, including cold emailing, cold calling, and prospecting in general. Let's dive into how to create a high-quality, well-targeted email lead list.

You'll also discover Alex Hormozi’s approach to building an ideal customer profile and crafting a solid lead list—because if you're targeting the wrong people, they won’t care about your special discount offer, impressive case study, or even your super-personalized subject line.

If you want to sell more and grow your business, by the end of this video, you'll know the #1 sales growth secret.

web.markdown:
# What I Learned From Alex Hormozi: Sales Training From the $100 Million Dollar Man

- [Ayodeji Awosika](https://thevectorimpact.com/author/ayodeji/)
- January 2nd, 2026

You might be familiar with his cult classic business book, “$100M Offers.” Or the fact that he’s built multiple deca-million-dollar businesses. Or maybe something resonated when you heard him speak about sales and business.

I’m talking about the one and only Alex Hormozi, of course.

I’ve used Alex Hormozi sales training in the form of books, YouTube, and podcast episodes to scale my business to $500,000 ARR. I’ll use his methods to scale to seven figures next year.

Now, I’m going to show you how to use Alex Homozi’s sales training philosophy to grow _your_ business. I’ll walk you through the best sales tips, business lessons, and frameworks he used to build multiple eight-figure companies. Let’s go.

## 1\. Give, give, give, ask

!["The longer you delay the ask, the more money you can make." -Alex Hormozi](https://thevectorimpact.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/alex-hormozi-sales-training-01.png)

If you have an audience, their level of trust in you goes up the longer you wait to ask for a sale; then, it dwindles when you do.

Of course you have to sell to your audience at some point, but the more strategic you are about the process, the more money you can make in the long term.

Alex says that if you want to get rich, you should be long-term greedy instead of short-term greedy.

Here’s what both look like.

**Short-term greedy:** You build an audience and sell them as many low-quality products as possible as fast as possible. You’ll make some sales, sure. But you’ll also turn off a huge portion of your audience in the process. You keep making quick offers to make quick money, and your audience gets burned out.

**Long-term greedy:** You build an audience and focus on providing value. You provide free content that is better than your competition’s paid product. You entertain, inspire, and educate your audience. By the time you have a product for sale, they think, “If I got so much value from the free content, I can only imagine what the paid product can deliver.” Then, you can sell a high-quality product at premium prices and make a ton of cash in one fell swoop.

Alex uses Gary Vaynerchuk as an example. Gary is a popular social media expert and agency owner who also creates content for people to show them how to run businesses and create content.

Gary’s primary source of income isn’t his B2C audience, so he mostly gives away content.

Every once in a while, though, he makes a big ask. When he launched a book, he asked hard.

You can squeeze your audience for tiny amounts of cash and burn them out. Or you can wait and make a huge ask that will make you rich forever.

## 2\. The offer is king

![graphic of alex hormozi's book "$100M Offers"](https://thevectorimpact.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/alex-hormozi-sales-training-02.png)

Alex’s book, “$100M Offers” is all about creating offers people can’t refuse.

The “offer” is everything they will get in exchange for giving you money.

If you want to create a killer offer, you have to understand the different components of providing value:

- **Dream outcome:** What’s the life-changing outcome your customer is trying to achieve in their life or business? Six-pack abs, an extra million a year in revenue, traffic to their website, or a healthy and happy relationship are all examples.
- **Perceived likelihood of achievement:** In the customer’s eyes, based both on their perception of themselves and whether or not you can help them, how likely do they think the dream outcome is?
- **Time delay:** How long will it take to achieve the dream outcome?
- **Effort and sacrifice:** People default to wanting to achieve the outcome in the easiest and fastest way possible.

The “value equation” goes like this:

**Dream outcome x perceived likelihood of achievement/time delay x effort and sacrifice = value.**

The most valuable products get you the dream outcome, guaranteed, as fast as possible with little to no effort.

Let’s say the dream outcome for your potential customer is losing weight and getting a six-pack.

Your prospect could buy a book, do all the workouts, diet, and train for months or years. The time delay is long, the perceived likelihood of achievement is low, and it takes a ton of effort and sacrifice (which is why someone might only pay $10 for a book on fitness).

Or, a person could hire a personal trainer who will tell them _exactly what workouts to do_, create a customized meal plan, and check in constantly to make sure they’re on track. People pay more for customized training because the likelihood goes up and the time delay and sacrifice go down.

Last, another option would be liposuction. The effort is zero, the time delay is non-existent, and the likelihood of achievement is 100%—which is why it costs upwards of $20,000.

So the question becomes—when looking at the available options—how can you make _your_ offer more valuable and stack the deck in your favor so you can charge higher prices?

### Creating your grand slam offer

Make a list of every single problem your potential customer has.

Next, create a list of solutions to those problems, and don’t limit yourself to whether or not you can do them. Just brainstorm everything you can think of.

For example, if you are helping people lose weight and they hate shopping for healthy food, you could go to the store and shop for them.

You probably wouldn’t do this, but the point of the exercise is to think about every way you can solve their problems.

Then, after that, you look for the most valuable things you can do with the least amount of effort.

Take the best ones and add them to your offer. Some examples are below.

**Done _for_ you services:** Alex ran a gym turnaround business where he would fly to your gym, create all the advertisements, personally sell the people who walked in the door, and sell your gym to full capacity. He’d only keep the initial month of sales and you’d keep all the rest. He would only get paid if you did. Pretty hard to say no to, right?

**Done _with_ you services:** Can you provide a way to do the work with the customer to help them achieve the goal? Instead of just selling someone a program on how to do something, you can give them coaching time, walk them through the process step by step, and personally deliver some parts of the process.

**Resources:** Can you create a resource that can make it easier to achieve the dream outcome? I coach writers to write and get paid. I provide blog post templates, checklists, and links to the best publications to submit their work, which saves them time and energy.

Structuring the offer is so important that he wrote an entire book I couldn’t fully cover here, but the takeaway is clear.

What you sell, how you deliver what you sell, and the way the offer is structured matter more than the process of selling it. Get the offer right, and sales will be ten times easier.

### Now it’s your turn

Grab a pen and paper and write down every single problem your potential client is struggling with. No problem is too small.

Wait a day and revisit your list.

For each problem, come up with a potential solution that you can add to your offer.

Narrow your list down to the components that provide the **most long-term value** with a small amount of **repeated effort and sacrifice to deliver.**

The component may be something that takes a ton of time, energy, and even money to build ONCE—but requires little to no effort to maintain for each new client.

The most extreme example of this would be something like software.

Last, turn the solution into copy you can use to sell your offer. The easiest way to do this is to add the words “how to.”

- Problem: Bad at sales
- Solution: Word-for-word sales script
- Copy: How to land clients (even if you suck at sales)

#### **Example**

One of my students is a health and vitality coach for men in their 40s and older. Here is his offer exercise:

| Problem | Solution | Copy |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Low energy throughout the day | Personalized nutrition and exercise plan | How to boost your energy levels and keep up with your kids all day long |
| Decreased libido | Hormone-balancing diet and targeted supplements | How to reignite your passion and feel like a newlywed again |
| Brain fog affecting work performance | Brain-boosting workout routines and cognitive exercises | How to sharpen your mind and crush it at work (while still having energy for family time) |
| Difficulty losing stubborn belly fat | Tailored HIIT workouts and metabolic reset program | How to finally shed that dad bod and feel confident shirtless at the beach |
| Lack of time for self-care | Time-efficient 20-minute workout plans | How to get fit in just 20 minutes a day (even with a busy dad schedule) |
| Poor sleep quality | Sleep optimization techniques and evening routines | How to wake up refreshed and ready to tackle both parenting and work challenges |
| Stress management struggles | Stress-busting workout routines and mindfulness practices | How to become a calm, cool, and collected dad (even when life gets chaotic) |
| Decreased strength and muscle mass | Progressive strength training program | How to build "superhero dad" strength that impresses your kids and boosts your confidence |
| Lack of motivation to exercise | Accountability system and goal-setting workshops | How to stay motivated and consistent with your fitness (even when you'd rather crash on the couch) |
| Poor eating habits due to busy lifestyle | Meal prep strategies and quick, healthy recipes | How to fuel your body like a pro athlete (without spending hours in the kitchen) |

## 3\. Presenting your offer as a “new opportunity”

#### !["Your customers must be wholly ignorant of the difficulties involved in their vast undertaking. Experience is a handicap." —Eric Hoffer](https://thevectorimpact.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/alex-hormozi-sales-training-06.png)

When crafting and selling your offer, it’s often more compelling to present it as a new opportunity rather than an improvement on existing solutions.

This approach can help you stand out in a crowded market and attract clients who are looking for something different.

Improvement offers sell _through_ pain. New opportunities sell _away_ from pain.

### Why people don’t want improvement offers

- **Improvement is hard:** Most people have tried to improve in the past and it didn’t work. Selling them a better way to do something they’ve already tried just reminds them of their pain.
- **Desire vs. ambition:** Everyone has desire, but very few people have **serious ambition**. Improvement offers are selling to overachieving, ambitious people (which eliminates the vast majority of potential buyers). With a new opportunity, they don’t know what the process will be, so they don’t have to go through the familiar pain to get the result.
- **Memories of poor past decisions:** If your followers need improvement, then they have to admit failure. For them to say yes, they have to admit their past efforts fell short. Nobody wants to admit they’re wrong. An improvement offer _forces_ them to admit they’re wrong.
- **Commodity pricing:** Selling improvement means you’re selling against dozens or hundreds of other competitors selling the _same thing_.

### Why new opportunities are better than improvements

1. **Novelty factor:** People are drawn to new and exciting concepts.
2. **Differentiation:** It sets you apart from competitors who are simply offering “better” versions of the same thing.
3. **Higher perceived value:** New opportunities often command higher prices because they’re unique.
4. **Enthusiasm:** Clients are more likely to get excited about a fresh approach

#### **Examples of “new opportunity” offers**

- [**The Movie Star Body Program**](https://movie-star-body.mykajabi.com/) → It promises a _new style of training_ that will give you a Hollywood actor physique with minimalist training, intermittent fasting (which means you can eat big meals full of carbs you love), and “reverse pyramid training” that helps you build muscle faster.
- **Introverted Alpha** → A system for meeting a girlfriend for shy men where you hone your attractiveness instead of using “pick up artist tactics.”
- **[Vendingpreneuer](https://vendingpreneurs.com/)** → A new style of passive income that many people haven’t tried before (vending machines).
- **Words to Dollars** → Helps writers monetize faster with high-ticket clients instead of wasting years writing Medium articles, publishing books, and other inefficient methods.

We’ve covered some foundational principles. Now, let’s get into some sales tactics.

## 4\. Apply the CLOSER framework

Alex Hormozi’s sales training is in the back of my mind during every sales call. He breaks it down into an easy-to-understand framework so you can easily walk through each step.

It’s called the C-L-O-S-E-R framework:

- **C** larify why they’re there
- **L** abel them with a problem
- **O** verview their past pain
- **S** ell them a vacation
- **E** xplain away their concerns
- **R** einforce their decision

### Clarify why they’re there

Your prospects are all on the call because they have a problem.

It’s your job to frame the conversation from the start, so that you have a productive sales call instead of giving away free advice.

I like to use this question:

> What are the pain points, problems, or challenges that led to you booking a call today?

Don’t let them get away with an answer like:

> I’m just looking for more information.

Nobody wastes their precious time going on random calls looking for information. You can bring this up in a light-teasing way and re-direct back to the question:

> Do you just sign up for random calls with people to get information (light-hearted joking tone)? You wouldn’t have spent valuable time speaking with me unless something was going on so, what _reall_ y brought you here today?

This question will naturally transition to the next phase of the sales process.

### Label them with a problem

Until you identify a problem that both you and the prospect agree is the root cause of them not getting the desired outcome, don’t move on to the next phase of the conversation.

After you ask the clarifying question at the beginning, you can use additional questions and statements to get to the root of the problem:

- Tell me more about that…
- Out of everything you mentioned what do you think is the number one obstacle to \[outcome\]?
- What do you think you need most to hit your goal?

Once you’ve clearly labeled the number one problem they’re facing, you can move to the next step.

Sales is about identifying the gap between where they are and where they want to be.

It starts with making your prospect feel understood.

If you meet them where they’re at and find the right problem—the thing that’s keeping them up at night—then you can move them to your solution because you first took the time to put yourself in their shoes.

### Overview their past pain

This probably isn’t the first time they’ve tried to get the problem solved.

You want to find out what they’ve already tried so you can emphasize what you do _differently_ and sell _that piece of it_ when it comes to the pitch.

Why?

People don’t want an improved version of a strategy they already tried and failed to implement.

Instead, present your offer as a different path to get what they want. This takes the blame away from _them_ and puts it on the _strategy,_ which will inspire them to try something new.

Ask them about everything they’ve tried to get the result.

Even better, ask them what they’ve spent money on to solve the problem. This will help you re-frame your offer and qualify them as a buyer. Buyers tend to keep buying more stuff to solve their problem—which means they are in an excellent position to take you up on your new offer.

### Sell them a vacation

Here’s the biggest mistake rookie salespeople make when trying to sell a product or service of any kind.

They focus on the features and the deliverables, instead of the desired outcome.

Take my world of online courses, coaching, and consulting.

Instead of talking about how many one-on-one calls they’ll get, how many hours of content are inside the program, or what the office hours are, I talk about the _outcomes_ they’ll get after working with me.

Here is an example of me “selling the vacation” when I pitch my offer to my potential students (who want to build online businesses):

> The first thing we’ll do is sit down one-on-one to package your knowledge and skills into an offer you can sell for $1,000/mo minimum, **so you can build a $10K/mo business.** That way you don’t need a big audience or have to work endless hours just to hit your income goals.
>
> Next, we’ll validate your offer by landing your first three high-ticket clients **so you can have full confidence your offer works because people opened up their wallets to buy it.**
>
> Last, we’ll give you content frameworks to attract clients and show you our sell-by-chat method so you can attract high-quality people who are willing and able to invest in your offer every single day, which will lead to adding $10K/mo recurring in six months.

Notice how my pitch is all about the outcomes they’ll get?

I talk a bit about the “how” and the “what” behind the outcome, but I spend most of my time selling the vacation.

### Explain away their concerns

[Objection handling](https://www.thevectorimpact.com/overcoming-sales-objections/) is a topic in and of itself which is why we included an entire section on it, but here’s what you have to understand about your sales prospects.

They want to buy. They want to believe you. They wouldn’t be there otherwise. It’s your job to help them.

They are afraid of making a mistake, feeling dumb, and lowering their status. They want to trust you, but maybe they’ve been burned before. They might trust you but don’t trust themselves because they’ve tried and failed.

They’re hurting, and it’s your job to ease the pain.

When you think of objection handling as more of a collaborative effort, this helps. You should [genuinely listen](https://www.thevectorimpact.com/passive-listening/) and explain away their concerns instead of taking a combative, argumentative approach where you have to convince them to buy. Embrace the collaborative mindset, and selling becomes ten times easier.

### Re-affirm their decision

Immediately after buying something, especially if it’s expensive, people can feel “buyer’s remorse.”

Excitement turns into fear. They start second-guessing themselves and questioning whether or not they made the right decision.

This is why it’s important to have a smooth onboarding process right after the transaction is complete.

In my case, I like to bring my community success manager onto the call right then and there. I do the introduction and “pass them off” so he can do a full walkthrough of the community.

I also take a bit of time to show them around if I close well before the next call.

I verbally affirm their decision right then and there. I have video modules in my courses that re-affirm them again before they get access to the meat and potatoes.

Continue to congratulate them on making a smart investment over and over again until they are fully integrated into your system, product, or service.

## 5\. Objection handling 101

![alex hormozi sales training handling sales objections](https://thevectorimpact.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/alex-hormozi-sales-training-03.png)

In sales, you have to learn [how to handle objections](https://www.thevectorimpact.com/overcoming-sales-objections/).

People usually don’t say yes right away.

You have to work with them to see the value in your product, help them believe it’s possible to achieve the outcome, and show them that you’re the right person to help them.

And so, before we dive deep into objection handling, it’s important to burn this rule into your brain.

### The conversation begins with no

I work with a lot of business owners, usually coaches and freelancers, and I’ve shared this lesson so that they can become more comfortable with closing.

If you can only close sales calls where the person instantly says yes and faces little resistance, you are going to fumble a ton of deals that you absolutely could have closed.

The distribution goes like this:

- 10% of people were going to buy no matter what
- 10% of people were never going to buy
- 80% of people are in the middle: curious and interested about your offer but not completely sold right away

Your ability to deal with that 80% affects how much your business will grow.

I have to teach my clients to _not_ be surprised when people say no.

I have to help them understand that prospects are going to stall, they’re going to say “I need to think about it,” and they’re going to say “I’m too busy” or “I can’t afford it.”

When you don’t have a ton of experience in sales, getting that no can rattle you. Often, because they feel uncomfortable [handling objections](https://thevectorimpact.com/overcoming-sales-objections/), the business owner will just say:

“Sounds good, take your time to think about it!”

And then the prospect ghosts and is never to be seen again. That on-call environment has the highest likelihood to get the result of a sale so it’s important to be a closer.

Everything that happens before you make the ask is sales and everything after is closing.

If you want to earn more, you have to learn [how to close](https://thevectorimpact.com/how-to-close-a-sale/).

Which reminds me of another sales lesson I learned from Alex.

### Closers have hard conversations

If your goal is to care about the prospect more than they care about themselves, sometimes that means having a tough conversation with them and getting them to face the obstacle head on.

Usually they will have been struggling with the same problem for months or even years. They haven’t resolved it because they are indecisive, or they rationalize their problems, and they always find a reason why now isn’t the right time.

👆🏾And this is why most human beings don’t achieve their biggest goals in life in general.

So, to break that pattern, sometimes a challenging conversation is required. If you’ve [built rapport](https://thevectorimpact.com/how-to-build-rapport-in-sales/) with a prospect you can say something like:

“Can I level with you here?”

And then instead of using the typical objection responses, you directly explain why they’ve struggled to get the result for so long. You help them see that things have to change, and they have to _make changes_ to change. Clinging to labels like “I’m just this type of person” or “this is the way I do things” doesn’t work if they want to have different and better results.

So if you rifle through the objection handles I am going to show you in the next section, know that the above option is available if you need it.

### Objection handling 101

**Objection:** I need time to think.

**Reply:** It doesn’t take time to make a decision; it takes information. I’m your source of information and you have me here right now. What are your concerns? Let’s talk about it so I can help you decide either way.

**Why this works:** Both parties understand that the real issue is the doubt, indecision, and fear that goes into buying. So, this brings the conversation back to whatever the real issue is so we can work it out.

* * *

**Objection:** I’m busy or don’t have time to commit to this.

**Reply:** Do you think you’ll be any less busy anytime soon? If not, you can’t achieve the outcome unless everything in your life lines up perfectly, which probably won’t happen. We make time for what’s important. If you can find a way to fit this into your life right now, imagine what your results will be like if time _does free up_. Let’s work together to find the time.

**Why this works:** It’s common sense and logical. If the person really wants the outcome, they’ll come to the understanding that they have to make it a priority. If they believe you can help them do that, they’ll buy.

* * *

**Objection:** I can’t afford it.

**Reply:** Totally get it. If you can’t afford it, you can’t afford it. But if the investment would just make things _tight for you_, here’s what I’d say. Often, not having money is the best reason to buy. If the investment stings a little, you’ll be more motivated to do the work, so you won’t have to be in a position like this forever.

**Why this works**: First, it only works if people do have the money. Sometimes they just don’t. But the underlying issue is always doubt, fear, and uncertainty. If you can flip the conversation in their mind and turn that financial sting into motivation, you can close the deal.

These are a few ways you can help people make decisions, which leads to the next point…

## 6\. Be a coach, not a salesperson

![alex hormozi sales training 04](https://thevectorimpact.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/alex-hormozi-sales-training-04.png)

If you want to get good at sales, you have to genuinely be there to _help_ people make a decision (instead of just trying to get them to buy).

Alex says that the sales process is coaching.

You’re coaching them to make the right decision, and sometimes the right decision isn’t to buy.

Sometimes you _will_ run into people who _aren’t_ good candidates for what you do.

Sometimes people _are_ too broke to work with you, which is why Alex says, “Solve rich people’s problems, they’ll pay more.”

If you accept anyone who walks in the door with a credit card, you might be running the wrong business.

I listened to a podcast once where he talked about the difference between scammy “make-money-online” gurus and universities.

#### **Make money online gurus:**

- Will take anyone’s money
- Don’t filter out bad prospects who will fail using their products
- Make promises of riches

* * *

#### **Ivy league university:**

- Rejects most applicants
- Makes zero-income promises
- Focuses on creating a stellar reputation where the results of the students speak for themselves

In the e-learning space, Alex promotes not taking money from just anyone or making promises to the moon.

Instead, he suggests working with the most qualified people who can actually succeed, charge a premium, and post their results instead of promising anything.

Most people won’t take this route because it’s harder. It takes more time. It means turning down opportunities for easy money.

**You can use this attitude to sell anything.**

Don’t take the easy way out and try to sell everything to everyone. When you’re trying to sell someone something, ask the right questions with the sole purpose of finding out if they’re a good fit.

If they are, you can push hard for the sale because you know it’s your duty to help them because you _can_ help them. If not, you can part ways.

Alex talks a lot about doing what others won’t: being patient, taking the time to build amazing products, and—most importantly—actually giving a damn about the people you’re trying to help.

That’s the true secret to sales success.

## 7\. Put up more shots

!["Do so much volume it would be unreasonable for you to suck." -Alex Hormozi](https://thevectorimpact.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/alex-hormozi-sales-training-05.png)

Alex put out fliers to get people to come to his gym. After putting up 500 of them, he was perplexed to find that nobody showed up. He went to a friend who had told him to post the fliers in the first place.

“This flier thing doesn’t work,” Alex said.

“How many fliers did you put up?” said the friend.

“500.”

“Dude, we put up **5,000 fliers** just for a test run before we send the real amount.”

When it comes to sales, most people don’t do nearly enough to be profitable. Most business owners should take the amount of time they spend on their sales efforts and 10 or 100x it.

Alex has also talked about this foolproof strategy for getting clients if you’re a freelancer or run an [online service business](https://www.thevectorimpact.com/steps-to-start-an-online-business/). It’s simple. Send **100 DMs or emails per day.**

If you do that long enough, you will have clients. Most freelancers won’t send 100 DMs in their entire careers, which is why they’re broke.

Alex says he learned the nuances of sales by personally doing 4,000 sales calls.

**Most people who try to succeed in business don’t do enough reps. Not even close.**

They don’t do enough cold calls, they don’t [send enough messages](https://www.thevectorimpact.com/cold-pitching/), they don’t [create enough content](https://www.thevectorimpact.com/why-you-must-start-creating-content/), and they don’t go through the grueling process of learning to sell by practicing a ton.

Most sales problems, most business problems, and most life problems are effort problems.

The biggest lesson I learned from Alex is that you have to be willing to do the boring work [over and over and over again](https://www.thevectorimpact.com/incremental-change/) until you finally win.

Do _that_ and sales won’t be an issue.

## 8\. The most important sales lesson (that isn’t a sales lesson)

![Alex Hormozi quote: "Doing the basics at scale is what makes you advanced, not complexity."](https://thevectorimpact.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/alex-hormozi-sales-training-08.png)

One of the most important lessons I have learned from Alex is that the best strategy for your business is, more often than not, simply sticking with it.

If there’s one skill that’s both profitable and difficult to learn, it’s the skill of continuing the boring fundamentals, sticking with the strategy you already have, and avoiding shiny objects.

Let’s look at advertising for example, which, according to Alex, simply means letting people know about your stuff.

In his book “$100M leads,” Alex shares four ways to reach people:

- **Content:** You’re promoting your stuff one to many without paying
- **Paid advertisements:** You’re promoting your stuff one to many, but paid
- **Warm outreach:** You’re reaching out one-on-one to people who already know you
- **Cold outreach:** You’re reaching out one-on-one to people who don’t know you

Most business owners are reactive. When they run into a slump in sales, their instinct is to switch to an entirely new channel.

Alex doesn’t say you can’t make the switch (because sometimes it IS necessary), but he says that every time you make a big pivot, **it better be worth it.** Because this will move you 20% backwards before you make a leap forward. And that’s only a trade you can make so many times.

I’m referencing this video that hit me at the just the right time (worth the watch):

![YouTube video player](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/-UzJOk85OZI/hqdefault.jpg)

### How slacking on fundamentals played out in my business

The business I run relies on two methods:

- Creating organic content
- Warm outreach

A year ago, I was firing on all cylinders. Over the summer and into early fall, I hit a slump. It was partially because I spent the summer partying more than working. It was also because I became sloppy and wasn’t giving my all to the fundamentals.

Also, I made too many pivots. I hired and fired someone within three months, because I was impatient with the returns I was getting.

Video marketing has always been a good channel for me, but from time to time I abandon it for no other reason than boredom and shiny object syndrome. During the summer I stopped being consistent. I wasn’t sending new emails to my list. I was repurposing old ones or not sending them at all.

I wasn’t using all the tools in my marketing arsenal that I’d usually use like list promos, direct calls to action on social media, and webinars—all things that were working well. I let my business grind to a halt. To kick it back into gear, I had an amazing thought:

“I know, I’ll start running paid ads!”

I bought a $5,000 course on ads. I got halfway through it before I watched the Hormozi video and realized that I needed to get back to the basics. Do the same model, but do it well.

Lo and behold, after the first week I got consistent sending DMs, I landed a client within a day. I started taking the time to write brand new emails, improving the messaging, and what do you know—engagement went up. I worked on creating different entry points into my core offer by making small offers, which was a part of my original strategy, too.

And I’ve only scratched the surface of what’s possible with organic and warm outreach. Will I make the paid ads trade? Maybe, sometime in the future, but it doesn’t make sense until the foundation of my business is where it needs to be.

* * *

People get [a lot of things wrong about working in sales](https://thevectorimpact.com/working-in-sales/). Alex would tell you it’s not about persuasion tricks. It’s about building things worth buying, caring enough to tell people the truth, and doing the unsexy work long after most people get bored and quit. The trust you build, the offers you design, the reps you put in, and the fundamentals you practice—all of those things compound in ways that are hard to see in the moment, but impossible to ignore over time.

[![](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/560ac6222d6452bb11318794a03be20c4b1237189be3b6a0d97d42bc506ab9dd?s=300&d=blank&r=g)](https://thevectorimpact.com/author/ayodeji/)

[Ayodeji Awosika](https://thevectorimpact.com/author/ayodeji/)

Ayodeji Awosika is the author of the best-selling book, The Destiny Formula. A freelance writer and coach, he helps aspiring writers turn pro.

web.markdown:
![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/k834E6OTGTM/maxresdefault.jpg?v=64e0fea8)
# [$100M Leads Launch](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k834E6OTGTM)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Alex Hormozi](http://www.youtube.com/@AlexHormozi)
**Uploaded at**: 2023-08-19T10:45:13-07:00
**Published at**: 2023-08-19T10:45:13-07:00
**Length**: 01:27:39
**Views**: 1064390
**Likes**: 32345
**Category**: Education

## Description

```
Want to SCALE your business? Go here: https://acquisition.com 
Want to START a business? Go here: https://skool.com/games 

If you’re new to my channel, my name is Alex Hormozi. I’m the founder and managing partner of Acquisition.com. It’s a family office, which is just a formal way of saying we invest our own money into companies. Our 10 portfolio companies bring in over $200,000,000+ per year. Our ownership stake varies between 20% and 100% of them. Given this is a YT channel, and anyone can claim anything, I’ll give you some stuff you can google to verify below.

How I got here…

21: Graduated Vanderbilt in 3 years Magna Cum Laude, and took a fancy consulting job.
23 yrs old: Left my fancy consulting job to start a business (a gym).
24 yrs old: Opened 5 gym locations.
26 yrs old: Closed down 6th gym. Lost everything. 
26 yrs old: Got back to launching gyms (launched 33). Then, lost everything for a 2nd time. 
26 yrs old: In desperation, started licensing model as a hail mary. It worked. 
27 yrs old: "Gym Launch" does $3M profit the next 6 months. Then $17M profit next 12 months.
28 yrs old: Started Prestige Labs. $20M the first year.  
29 yrs old: Launched ALAN, a software company for agencies to work leads for customers. Scaled to $1.7mmo within 6 months. 
31 yrs old: Sold 75% of UseAlan to a strategic buyer in an all stock deal. 
31 yrs old: Sold 66% of Gym Launch & Prestige Labs at $46.2M valuation in all-cash deal to American Pacific Group. (you can google it)
31 yrs old: Started our family office Acquisition.com. We invest and scale companies using the $42M in distributions we had taken + the cash from the $46.2M exit.
32 yrs old: Started making free content showing how we grow companies to make real business education accessible to everyone (and) to attract business owners to invest or scale their businesses. 
34 yrs old: I became co-owner of https://Skool.com to help the many people who want to start a business online do so.

Today: Our portfolio now does $200M/yr between 10 companies. The largest doing $100M/yr the smallest doing $5M per year. Our ownership varies between 20% and 100% ownership of the companies. Many of them we invested in early and helped grow (which is how we make our money - not youtube videos).

To all the gladiators in the arena, we’re all in the middle of writing our own stories. The worse the monsters, the more epic the story.

You either get an epic outcome or an epic story. Both mean you win. 

Keep crushing. May your desires be greater than your obstacles. 

Never quit,

Alex

*FULL DISCLOSURE* 
I make content to make money - just - on a longer time horizon than most. I want to build trust with business owners so we can find the best ones and help them scale. And if they’re awesome, write them a check and go all the way as partners.
```

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:64:64/1*dmbNkD5D-u45r44go_cf0g.png)

# How Alex Hormozi Turned a Webinar into a $100M Money Machine

[![Alexandru Rada](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:64:64/1*qQJb6AxkFh8EoqvH7JkSZQ.png)](https://medium.com/@alexandrurada_346?source=post_page---byline--0fb727e3a3ee---------------------------------------)

[Alexandru Rada](https://medium.com/@alexandrurada_346?source=post_page---byline--0fb727e3a3ee---------------------------------------)

2 min read

·

Aug 20, 2025

--

Listen

Share

Alex Hormozi doesn’t just release books — he bundles them into spectacle. On **August 16, 2025**, he hosted a **10-hour YouTube livestream** dubbed the **“$100M Money Models Virtual Live Book Launch,”** and it was nothing short of a marketing masterclass.

[Alex Hormozi Linktree on Lovable](https://alex-hormozi-linktree-hub.lovable.app/)

## Reframing the Pitch: From “Buy a Book” to “Donate to Multiply Impact”

Hormozi didn’t ask people to buy his book. He urged them to **“donate” 200 copies** — one for themselves, 199 to share. Priced at **$5,998**, this twist on generosity triggered a viral loop: recipients often shared codes with their networks, capturing leads while amplifying the launch’s reach [Startup Spells](https://startupspells.com/p/alex-hormozi-100m-money-models-launch-donate-200-books-record-day-one-sales?utm_source=chatgpt.com).

Press enter or click to view image in full size

## A Value Buffet That Converts

What did this generous act unlock? A massive value stack:

- **12 premium physical playbooks** in a branded binder — covering topics like closing, retention, branding, and ad strategy.
- The hardcover of **_100M Money Models_** plus **199 redemption codes** for others (shipping covered by recipients).
- Early access to the accompanying course, audiobook, and a live virtual **Implementation Workshop**.
- **ACQ AI**, a chatbot trained on Hormozi’s wisdom, to simulate what Alex would do in your business.
- Access to Skool, the community platform — with mentions of a **$9/month** fee for live attendees [Acquisition+11Startup Spells+11Instagram+11](https://startupspells.com/p/alex-hormozi-100m-money-models-launch-donate-200-books-record-day-one-sales?utm_source=chatgpt.com).

## The Upsell Within the Funnel

Hormozi layered in scarcity and community-building: right after the 200-book bundle was an **800-copy upsell** tied to a six-month **$3,000/month advisory program** — a total of $18,000 for access to live intensives and direct guidance [Startup Spells+1](https://startupspells.com/p/alex-hormozi-100m-money-models-launch-donate-200-books-record-day-one-sales?utm_source=chatgpt.com).

## A Meta Launch: Practicing What He Preaches

This launch wasn’t just a marketing move — it was the embodiment of the book’s premise. Hormozi called each of his titles a **“meta book.”** Just as **_100M Offers_** launched with a free course and **_100M Leads_** with omnichannel distribution, this launch taught monetization by doing it live [Facebook+10Startup Spells+10AmpiFire+10](https://startupspells.com/p/alex-hormozi-100m-money-models-launch-donate-200-books-record-day-one-sales?utm_source=chatgpt.com).

## Results That Speak Louder Than Words

- The campaign reportedly surged into the **tens of millions** — with claims of **$81 million in a single day**, marking the biggest 24-hour non-fiction sale ever [Startup Spells](https://startupspells.com/p/alex-hormozi-100m-money-models-launch-donate-200-books-record-day-one-sales?utm_source=chatgpt.com).
- The strategy created a viral flywheel: social shares, affiliate leaderboards, and public giveaways fueled both reach and credibility [Startup Spells](https://startupspells.com/p/alex-hormozi-100m-money-models-launch-donate-200-books-record-day-one-sales?utm_source=chatgpt.com).

## Why This Worked (And Why It’s Worth a Read)

- **Generosity framed as investment**: “Donate to receive” triggers reciprocity and social proof.
- **High perceived value**: The bundle is rich with tangible (playbooks) and intangible (AI coach, workshop) assets.
- **Scarcity & urgency**: Fixed deadline (bundle closed August 18, 11:59 pm PT) and one-time upsells.
- **Game mechanics**: Public affiliate leaderboards turned launch into friendly competition and social proof.
- **Live demonstration of thesis**: The launch itself was the book in action.
